content.text: Let me explore the key architecture files to understand your FlyingMonkeys orchestration and agent capabilities:

content.content:
1→# Complete Agent Catalog
     2→
     3→This document provides a comprehensive list of all 44 agents included in the Claude Code Agents system.
     4→
     5→## Summary
     6→
     7→- **Total Agents**: 44
     8→- **Categories**: 7
     9→- **Lines of Code**: ~8,000+
    10→- **TypeScript Files**: 50+
    11→
    12→## Category Breakdown
    13→
    14→| Category | Agent Count | Focus Area |
    15→|----------|-------------|------------|
    16→| Product Strategy | 5 | Product direction, growth, monetization |
    17→| Development | 11 | Architecture, code quality, APIs, performance |
    18→| Design & UX | 5 | User experience, UI polish, content |
    19→| Quality & Testing | 5 | Testing, security, code review |
    20→| Operations | 7 | DevOps, infrastructure, monitoring |
    21→| Business & Analytics | 8 | Analytics, marketing, compliance |
    22→| AI & Innovation | 3 | AI integration, automation, R&D |
    23→
    24→## Complete Agent List
    25→
    26→### Product Strategy Agents (5)
    27→
    28→1. **Product Strategist** (`product-strategist`)
    29→   - Analyzes features and asks hard questions
    30→   - Tells you what to build next and what to kill
    31→   - Focus: Strategy, roadmap, prioritization
    32→
    33→2. **Growth Engineer** (`growth-engineer`)
    34→   - Finds where users get hooked
    35→   - Builds viral loops that actually work
    36→   - Focus: Growth, viral mechanics, retention
    37→
    38→3. **User Researcher** (`user-researcher`)
    39→   - Analyzes actual user flows
    40→   - Shows where people rage quit and fixes it
    41→   - Focus: UX research, friction elimination
    42→
    43→4. **Revenue Optimizer** (`revenue-optimizer`)
    44→   - Spots money-making opportunities
    45→   - Implements pricing tiers and payment flows
    46→   - Focus: Monetization, payments, conversion
    47→
    48→5. **Market Analyst** (`market-analyst`)
    49→   - Compares features to competitors
    50→   - Finds your unfair advantages
    51→   - Focus: Competitive analysis, positioning
    52→
    53→### Development Agents (11)
    54→
    55→6. **System Architect** (`system-architect`)
    56→   - Transforms messy codebases into clean systems
    57→   - Your future self will thank you
    58→   - Focus: Architecture, design patterns, scalability
    59→
    60→7. **Code Refactorer** (`code-refactorer`)
    61→   - Cleans up code written at 3am
    62→   - Makes it readable, fast, maintainable
    63→   - Focus: Refactoring, clean code, readability
    64→
    65→8. **API Builder** (`api-builder`)
    66→   - Creates beautiful APIs developers want to use
    67→   - Includes auth, rate limiting, docs
    68→   - Focus: REST, GraphQL, API design
    69→
    70→9. **Database Expert** (`database-expert`)
    71→   - Fixes queries that take 30 seconds
    72→   - Designs schemas that scale to millions
    73→   - Focus: SQL, NoSQL, query optimization
    74→
    75→10. **Integration Master** (`integration-master`)
    76→    - Connects your app to any service
    77→    - Handles auth flows, webhooks, retries
    78→    - Focus: Third-party APIs, integrations
    79→
    80→11. **Mobile Optimizer** (`mobile-optimizer`)
    81→    - Makes web apps feel native on phones
    82→    - Adds offline support, PWA features
    83→    - Focus: Mobile, PWA, responsive design
    84→
    85→12. **Performance Engineer** (`performance-engineer`)
    86→    - Finds the 5 lines making your app slow
    87→    - Implements caching that actually works
    88→    - Focus: Performance, optimization, profiling
    89→
    90→13. **Accessibility Pro** (`accessibility-pro`)
    91→    - Makes your app work for everyone
    92→    - Screen readers, keyboard nav, WCAG compliance
    93→    - Focus: Accessibility, a11y, WCAG
    94→
    95→14. **Microservices Architect** (`microservices-architect`)
    96→    - Breaks monoliths into microservices
    97→    - Designs service boundaries and communication
    98→    - Focus: Microservices, distributed systems
    99→
   100→15. **GraphQL Expert** (`graphql-expert`)
   101→    - Builds type-safe GraphQL APIs
   102→    - Optimal resolver patterns, federation
   103→    - Focus: GraphQL, Apollo, schema design
   104→
   105→16. **State Management Expert** (`state-management-expert`)
   106→    - Tames complex state management
   107→    - Redux, Zustand, React Query patterns
   108→    - Focus: State management, data flow
   109→
   110→### Design & UX Agents (5)
   111→
   112→17. **UX Optimizer** (`ux-optimizer`)
   113→    - Simplifies confusing user flows
   114→    - Reduces 10 clicks to 2
   115→    - Focus: UX, user flows, simplification
   116→
   117→18. **UI Polisher** (`ui-polisher`)
   118→    - Makes your app look expensive
   119→    - Adds animations, micro-interactions
   120→    - Focus: UI polish, animations, design
   121→
   122→19. **Content Writer** (`content-writer`)
   123→    - Turns boring error messages into helpful guides
   124→    - Makes every word work harder
   125→    - Focus: UX writing, microcopy, messaging
   126→
   127→20. **Design System Builder** (`design-system-builder`)
   128→    - Creates component libraries you'll use
   129→    - Consistent styles across everything
   130→    - Focus: Design systems, components, tokens
   131→
   132→21. **Technical Writer** (`technical-writer`)
   133→    - Writes documentation developers read
   134→    - Clear examples, no jargon, searchable
   135→    - Focus: Documentation, technical writing
   136→
   137→### Quality & Testing Agents (5)
   138→
   139→22. **Test Generator** (`test-generator`)
   140→    - Writes the tests you've been avoiding
   141→    - Unit, integration, E2E tests
   142→    - Focus: Testing, Jest, Playwright, coverage
   143→
   144→23. **Security Scanner** (`security-scanner`)
   145→    - Finds vulnerabilities before hackers
   146→    - Implements auth, validation, protection
   147→    - Focus: Security, OWASP, vulnerabilities
   148→
   149→24. **Code Reviewer** (`code-reviewer`)
   150→    - Reviews code like a senior engineer
   151→    - Catches bugs, suggests improvements
   152→    - Focus: Code review, quality, best practices
   153→
   154→25. **Load Tester** (`load-tester`)
   155→    - Simulates 10,000 users hitting your app
   156→    - Finds breaking points and fixes them
   157→    - Focus: Load testing, performance, scalability
   158→
   159→26. **Documentation Generator** (`documentation-generator`)
   160→    - Generates comprehensive code documentation
   161→    - JSDoc, docstrings, inline comments
   162→    - Focus: Documentation, JSDoc, code comments
   163→
   164→### Operations Agents (7)
   165→
   166→27. **Deployment Wizard** (`deployment-wizard`)
   167→    - Sets up CI/CD that actually works
   168→    - Push to main, deploy to production

# Complete Agent Catalog

This document provides a comprehensive list of all 44 agents included in the Claude Code Agents system.

## Summary

- **Total Agents**: 44
- **Categories**: 7
- **Lines of Code**: ~8,000+
- **TypeScript Files**: 50+

## Category Breakdown

| Category | Agent Count | Focus Area |
|----------|-------------|------------|
| Product Strategy | 5 | Product direction, growth, monetization |
| Development | 11 | Architecture, code quality, APIs, performance |
| Design & UX | 5 | User experience, UI polish, content |
| Quality & Testing | 5 | Testing, security, code review |
| Operations | 7 | DevOps, infrastructure, monitoring |
| Business & Analytics | 8 | Analytics, marketing, compliance |
| AI & Innovation | 3 | AI integration, automation, R&D |

## Complete Agent List

### Product Strategy Agents (5)

1. **Product Strategist** (`product-strategist`)
   - Analyzes features and asks hard questions
   - Tells you what to build next and what to kill
   - Focus: Strategy, roadmap, prioritization

2. **Growth Engineer** (`growth-engineer`)
   - Finds where users get hooked
   - Builds viral loops that actually work
   - Focus: Growth, viral mechanics, retention

3. **User Researcher** (`user-researcher`)
   - Analyzes actual user flows
   - Shows where people rage quit and fixes it
   - Focus: UX research, friction elimination

4. **Revenue Optimizer** (`revenue-optimizer`)
   - Spots money-making opportunities
   - Implements pricing tiers and payment flows
   - Focus: Monetization, payments, conversion

5. **Market Analyst** (`market-analyst`)
   - Compares features to competitors
   - Finds your unfair advantages
   - Focus: Competitive analysis, positioning

### Development Agents (11)

6. **System Architect** (`system-architect`)
   - Transforms messy codebases into clean systems
   - Your future self will thank you
   - Focus: Architecture, design patterns, scalability

7. **Code Refactorer** (`code-refactorer`)
   - Cleans up code written at 3am
   - Makes it readable, fast, maintainable
   - Focus: Refactoring, clean code, readability

8. **API Builder** (`api-builder`)
   - Creates beautiful APIs developers want to use
   - Includes auth, rate limiting, docs
   - Focus: REST, GraphQL, API design

9. **Database Expert** (`database-expert`)
   - Fixes queries that take 30 seconds
   - Designs schemas that scale to millions
   - Focus: SQL, NoSQL, query optimization

10. **Integration Master** (`integration-master`)
    - Connects your app to any service
    - Handles auth flows, webhooks, retries
    - Focus: Third-party APIs, integrations

11. **Mobile Optimizer** (`mobile-optimizer`)
    - Makes web apps feel native on phones
    - Adds offline support, PWA features
    - Focus: Mobile, PWA, responsive design

12. **Performance Engineer** (`performance-engineer`)
    - Finds the 5 lines making your app slow
    - Implements caching that actually works
    - Focus: Performance, optimization, profiling

13. **Accessibility Pro** (`accessibility-pro`)
    - Makes your app work for everyone
    - Screen readers, keyboard nav, WCAG compliance
    - Focus: Accessibility, a11y, WCAG

14. **Microservices Architect** (`microservices-architect`)
    - Breaks monoliths into microservices
    - Designs service boundaries and communication
    - Focus: Microservices, distributed systems

15. **GraphQL Expert** (`graphql-expert`)
    - Builds type-safe GraphQL APIs
    - Optimal resolver patterns, federation
    - Focus: GraphQL, Apollo, schema design

16. **State Management Expert** (`state-management-expert`)
    - Tames complex state management
    - Redux, Zustand, React Query patterns
    - Focus: State management, data flow

### Design & UX Agents (5)

17. **UX Optimizer** (`ux-optimizer`)
    - Simplifies confusing user flows
    - Reduces 10 clicks to 2
    - Focus: UX, user flows, simplification

18. **UI Polisher** (`ui-polisher`)
    - Makes your app look expensive
    - Adds animations, micro-interactions
    - Focus: UI polish, animations, design

19. **Content Writer** (`content-writer`)
    - Turns boring error messages into helpful guides
    - Makes every word work harder
    - Focus: UX writing, microcopy, messaging

20. **Design System Builder** (`design-system-builder`)
    - Creates component libraries you'll use
    - Consistent styles across everything
    - Focus: Design systems, components, tokens

21. **Technical Writer** (`technical-writer`)
    - Writes documentation developers read
    - Clear examples, no jargon, searchable
    - Focus: Documentation, technical writing

### Quality & Testing Agents (5)

22. **Test Generator** (`test-generator`)
    - Writes the tests you've been avoiding
    - Unit, integration, E2E tests
    - Focus: Testing, Jest, Playwright, coverage

23. **Security Scanner** (`security-scanner`)
    - Finds vulnerabilities before hackers
    - Implements auth, validation, protection
    - Focus: Security, OWASP, vulnerabilities

24. **Code Reviewer** (`code-reviewer`)
    - Reviews code like a senior engineer
    - Catches bugs, suggests improvements
    - Focus: Code review, quality, best practices

25. **Load Tester** (`load-tester`)
    - Simulates 10,000 users hitting your app
    - Finds breaking points and fixes them
    - Focus: Load testing, performance, scalability

26. **Documentation Generator** (`documentation-generator`)
    - Generates comprehensive code documentation
    - JSDoc, docstrings, inline comments
    - Focus: Documentation, JSDoc, code comments

### Operations Agents (7)

27. **Deployment Wizard** (`deployment-wizard`)
    - Sets up CI/CD that actually works
    - Push to main, deploy to production
    - Focus: CI/CD, deployment, automation

28. **Infrastructure Builder** (`infrastructure-builder`)
    - Designs cloud architecture that scales
    - Terraform included, cost-optimized
    - Focus: Infrastructure, Terraform, AWS/GCP

29. **Monitoring Expert** (`monitoring-expert`)
    - Knows when your app breaks before users
    - Sets up alerts, logs, dashboards
    - Focus: Monitoring, observability, alerting

30. **Release Manager** (`release-manager`)
    - Handles deployments without downtime
    - Feature flags, rollbacks, smooth releases
    - Focus: Releases, feature flags, rollback

31. **Cost Optimizer** (`cost-optimizer`)
    - Cuts your AWS bill by 50%
    - Finds waste, right-sizes everything
    - Focus: Cost optimization, FinOps, savings

32. **DevOps Engineer** (`devops-engineer`)
    - Automates everything
    - Builds pipelines, manages infrastructure
    - Focus: DevOps, automation, SRE

33. **Dependency Manager** (`dependency-manager`)
    - Keeps dependencies up-to-date and secure
    - Automates updates and vulnerability scanning
    - Focus: Dependencies, security, updates

### Business & Analytics Agents (8)

34. **Analytics Engineer** (`analytics-engineer`)
    - Tracks what actually matters
    - User behavior, conversion funnels, insights
    - Focus: Analytics, tracking, metrics

35. **Email Automator** (`email-automator`)
    - Builds email flows users open
    - Welcome series, re-engagement, transactional
    - Focus: Email automation, campaigns

36. **Support Builder** (`support-builder`)
    - Creates help systems that reduce tickets 80%
    - FAQs, chat widgets, documentation
    - Focus: Support, help, customer success

37. **Compliance Expert** (`compliance-expert`)
    - Handles GDPR, CCPA, cookies
    - Keeps you legal without lawyer bills
    - Focus: Compliance, GDPR, privacy

38. **SEO Master** (`seo-master`)
    - Makes Google love your site
    - Meta tags, schema markup, Core Web Vitals
    - Focus: SEO, search, web vitals

39. **Community Features** (`community-features`)
    - Adds forums, comments, user profiles
    - Builds features that keep users coming back
    - Focus: Community, social, engagement

40. **Landing Page Optimizer** (`landing-page-optimizer`)
    - Writes copy that converts visitors
    - Headlines, CTAs, social proof that works
    - Focus: Conversion, copywriting, CRO

41. **A/B Testing Specialist** (`ab-testing-specialist`)
    - Runs experiments to optimize conversion
    - Tests headlines, CTAs, layouts
    - Focus: A/B testing, experiments, optimization

### AI & Innovation Agents (3)

42. **AI Integration Expert** (`ai-integration-expert`)
    - Adds ChatGPT-like features to your app
    - Handles prompts, streaming, embeddings
    - Focus: AI, LLMs, OpenAI, Anthropic

43. **Automation Builder** (`automation-builder`)
    - Automates the repetitive stuff
    - Scheduled jobs, workflows, triggers
    - Focus: Automation, workflows, cron jobs

44. **Innovation Lab** (`innovation-lab`)
    - Experiments with cutting-edge tech
    - Tries crazy ideas so you don't have to
    - Focus: Innovation, R&D, emerging tech

## Usage by Difficulty

### Beginner (6 agents)
- Content Writer
- Documentation Generator
- Email Automator
- Support Builder
- Landing Page Optimizer
- Dependency Manager

### Intermediate (20 agents)
- Product Strategist
- User Researcher
- Code Refactorer
- API Builder
- Integration Master
- Mobile Optimizer
- Accessibility Pro
- UX Optimizer
- UI Polisher
- Technical Writer
- Test Generator
- Security Scanner
- Code Reviewer
- Deployment Wizard
- Monitoring Expert
- Cost Optimizer
- Analytics Engineer
- Compliance Expert
- SEO Master
- Community Features
- A/B Testing Specialist
- Automation Builder

### Advanced (13 agents)
- Growth Engineer
- Revenue Optimizer
- Market Analyst
- Database Expert
- Performance Engineer
- GraphQL Expert
- State Management Expert
- Design System Builder
- Load Tester
- Release Manager
- AI Integration Expert

### Expert (5 agents)
- System Architect
- Microservices Architect
- Infrastructure Builder
- DevOps Engineer
- Innovation Lab

## Integration Patterns

All agents follow consistent patterns:

1. **Execution**: Via `execute(context)` method
2. **Results**: Structured output with artifacts, metrics, recommendations
3. **Configuration**: Customizable via AgentConfig
4. **Tools**: Predefined required and optional tool sets
5. **Workflows**: Multi-step execution with validation

## Next Steps

1. Install dependencies: `npm install`
2. Build the project: `npm run build`
3. Explore the agents in `src/agents/`
4. Read the [README.md](./README.md) for usage examples
5. Check out the [Claude Agent SDK docs](https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview)

---

Last updated: 2025-11-15

content.content:
1→# Master Agent Framework
     2→
     3→## Universal Agent Distribution Architecture
     4→
     5→A production-ready framework for distributing specialized AI agent configurations inspired by Claude Code's plugin marketplace architecture. This framework enables instant agent specialization, team-wide standardization, and version-controlled evolution of agent capabilities.
     6→
     7→## Table of Contents
     8→
     9→- [Overview](#overview)
    10→- [Architecture](#architecture)
    11→- [Core Concepts](#core-concepts)
    12→- [Agent Archetypes](#agent-archetypes)
    13→- [Installation & Usage](#installation--usage)
    14→- [Configuration](#configuration)
    15→- [Extension Development](#extension-development)
    16→- [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Overview
    21→
    22→### Vision
    23→
    24→**"Think Different. Make a Dent in the Universe."**
    25→
    26→The Master Agent Framework transforms how teams deploy and manage AI agents by providing:
    27→
    28→- **Instant Specialization**: Select agent archetypes from a marketplace for immediate deployment
    29→- **Team Standardization**: Share agent configurations across teams via version-controlled manifests
    30→- **Modular Composition**: Combine tools, personas, hooks, and integrations flexibly
    31→- **Production Ready**: Battle-tested patterns that "just work" at scale
    32→
    33→### Key Benefits
    34→
    35→1. **Marketplace-Driven Distribution**: Centralized discovery and installation of agent templates
    36→2. **Component Architecture**: Modular design with commands, agents, hooks, and MCP servers
    37→3. **Flexible Deployment**: Strict mode for production, non-strict for rapid prototyping
    38→4. **Dynamic Configuration**: Environment variable patterns for portable agent setups
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## Architecture
    43→
    44→### Component Model
    45→
    46→```
    47→master-agent-marketplace/
    48→├── marketplace/
    49→│   └── marketplace.json           # Central marketplace configuration
    50→├── agents/
    51→│   ├── research/                  # Research agent archetype
    52→│   │   ├── manifest.json          # Agent configuration
    53→│   │   ├── tools/                 # Agent-specific tools
    54→│   │   │   ├── search.js
    55→│   │   │   ├── synthesis.js
    56→│   │   │   └── citation.js
    57→│   │   ├── personas/              # Persona configurations
    58→│   │   │   ├── academic.md
    59→│   │   │   ├── business.md
    60→│   │   │   └── technical.md
    61→│   │   └── hooks/                 # Event-driven behaviors
    62→│   │       └── validate-sources.sh
    63→│   ├── coding/                    # Coding agent archetype
    64→│   │   ├── manifest.json
    65→│   │   ├── tools/
    66→│   │   │   ├── test-runner.js
    67→│   │   │   ├── linter.js
    68→│   │   │   └── formatter.js
    69→│   │   ├── personas/
    70→│   │   │   ├── backend-dev.md
    71→│   │   │   ├── frontend-dev.md
    72→│   │   │   └── devops.md
    73→│   │   └── hooks/
    74→│   │       ├── run-tests.sh
    75→│   │       └── lint-and-format.sh
    76→│   ├── analysis/                  # Analysis agent archetype
    77→│   └── deployment/                # Deployment agent archetype
    78→└── settings.json                  # Team-wide settings
    79→```
    80→
    81→### Architecture Principles
    82→
    83→1. **Marketplace-Centric**: All agents are distributed through a marketplace model
    84→2. **Component-Based**: Agents compose tools, personas, hooks, and integrations
    85→3. **Version-Controlled**: All configurations tracked in git for auditability
    86→4. **Environment-Aware**: Dynamic paths via `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` pattern
    87→5. **Mode-Flexible**: Strict mode for production, non-strict for development
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→## Core Concepts
    92→
    93→### 1. Agent Archetypes
    94→
    95→Pre-configured agent templates optimized for specific domains:
    96→
    97→- **Research Agent**: Information gathering, synthesis, citation management
    98→- **Coding Agent**: Software development, testing, code review
    99→- **Analysis Agent**: Data analysis, visualization, reporting
   100→- **Deployment Agent**: CI/CD, infrastructure, monitoring
   101→
   102→### 2. Component Types
   103→
   104→#### Commands (Tools)
   105→JavaScript/TypeScript implementations using Claude Agent SDK's `tool()` function:
   106→
   107→```typescript
   108→import { tool } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";
   109→
   110→export const myTool = tool({
   111→  name: "tool_name",
   112→  description: "What the tool does",
   113→  parameters: { /* JSON Schema */ },
   114→  execute: async (params) => { /* implementation */ }
   115→});
   116→```
   117→
   118→#### Personas
   119→Markdown files defining agent personality, expertise, and behavior:
   120→
   121→```markdown
   122→# Senior Backend Developer
   123→
   124→## Identity
   125→You are a senior backend developer...
   126→
   127→## Core Competencies
   128→- API design
   129→- Database optimization
   130→...
   131→
   132→## Temperature: 0.3
   133→```
   134→
   135→#### Hooks
   136→Event-driven shell scripts triggered by agent actions:
   137→
   138→```json
   139→{
   140→  "hooks": {
   141→    "PostToolUse": [{"type": "command", "command": "validate.sh"}],
   142→    "PreCommit": [{"type": "command", "command": "lint.sh"}]
   143→  }
   144→}
   145→```
   146→
   147→#### MCP Servers
   148→Model Context Protocol integrations for external services:
   149→
   150→```json
   151→{
   152→  "mcpServers": {
   153→    "github": {
   154→      "command": "npx",
   155→      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
   156→    }
   157→  }
   158→}
   159→```
   160→
   161→### 3. Operating Modes
   162→
   163→#### Strict Mode
   164→- Agent manifest must be complete and valid
   165→- All dependencies explicitly declared
   166→- Suitable for production environments
   167→- Enabled: `"strictMode": true`
   168→
   169→#### Non-Strict Mode
   170→- Marketplace can provide missing configuration
   171→- Faster prototyping and iteration
   172→- Suitable for development
   173→- Enabled: `"strictMode": false` (default)
   174→
   175→---
   176→
   177→## Agent Archetypes
   178→
   179→### Research Agent
   180→
   181→**Purpose**: Comprehensive information gathering and synthesis
   182→
   183→**Capabilities**:
   184→- Multi-source search (web, academic, technical)
   185→- Information synthesis with citations
   186→- Source credibility evaluation
   187→- Multiple citation styles (APA, MLA, Chicago)
   188→
   189→**Personas**:
   190→- **Academic**: Rigorous research with peer-reviewed focus
   191→- **Business**: Market research and actionable insights
   192→- **Technical**: Deep technical evaluation and benchmarking
   193→
   194→**Use Cases**:
   195→- Literature reviews
   196→- Competitive intelligence
   197→- Technology evaluation
   198→- Market research
   199→
   200→**Installation**:
   201→```bash
   202→npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install research-agent
   203→```
   204→
   205→### Coding Agent
   206→
   207→**Purpose**: Software development, testing, and quality assurance
   208→
   209→**Capabilities**:
   210→- Code generation (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go)
   211→- Test automation (pytest, jest, mocha)
   212→- Code review and refactoring
   213→- CI/CD integration
   214→
   215→**Personas**:
   216→- **Backend Developer**: API design, database optimization
   217→- **Frontend Developer**: React/Vue/Angular, UX focus
   218→- **DevOps Engineer**: Infrastructure, deployment, monitoring
   219→
   220→**Use Cases**:
   221→- Feature development
   222→- Test suite creation
   223→- Code refactoring
   224→- Deployment automation
   225→
   226→**Installation**:
   227→```bash
   228→npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install coding-agent
   229→```
   230→
   231→### Analysis Agent
   232→
   233→**Purpose**: Data analysis, metrics tracking, and reporting
   234→
   235→**Capabilities**:
   236→- Data analysis and visualization
   237→- Metrics tracking and reporting
   238→- Business intelligence
   239→- Performance analysis
   240→
   241→**Personas**:
   242→- **Data Scientist**: Statistical analysis, ML
   243→- **Business Analyst**: KPIs, ROI analysis
   244→
   245→**Use Cases**:
   246→- Performance reporting
   247→- Data pipeline analysis
   248→- Business metrics tracking
   249→
   250→### Deployment Agent
   251→
   252→**Purpose**: Infrastructure management and deployment automation
   253→
   254→**Capabilities**:
   255→- Deployment automation
   256→- Infrastructure as Code
   257→- CI/CD pipeline management
   258→- Health monitoring
   259→
   260→**Personas**:
   261→- **DevOps Engineer**: Deployment automation
   262→- **SRE**: Reliability and monitoring
   263→
   264→**Use Cases**:
   265→- Production deployments
   266→- Infrastructure provisioning
   267→- Monitoring setup
   268→
   269→---
   270→
   271→## Installation & Usage
   272→
   273→### Prerequisites
   274→
   275→```bash
   276→# Install Claude Agent SDK
   277→npm install @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
   278→# or
   279→pip install claude-agent-sdk
   280→```
   281→
   282→### Quick Start
   283→
   284→#### 1. Clone or Install from Marketplace
   285→
   286→```bash
   287→# Clone repository
   288→git clone https://github.com/yourusername/master-agent-framework.git
   289→cd master-agent-framework
   290→
   291→# Install dependencies
   292→npm install
   293→```
   294→
   295→#### 2. Select Agent Archetype
   296→
   297→```bash
   298→# Install research agent
   299→npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install research-agent
   300→
   301→# Or use the marketplace CLI (conceptual)
   302→./marketplace install research-agent@latest
   303→```
   304→
   305→#### 3. Configure Agent
   306→
   307→```json
   308→{
   309→  "agent": "research-agent",
   310→  "persona": "academic",
   311→  "tools": ["search", "synthesis", "citation"],
   312→  "settingSources": ["user", "project", "local"]
   313→}
   314→```
   315→
   316→#### 4. Use in Your Code
   317→
   318→**TypeScript Example**:
   319→
   320→```typescript
   321→import { query } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";
   322→import { searchTool, synthesisTool } from "./agents/research/tools";
   323→
   324→// Initialize agent with research tools
   325→const result = await query({
   326→  prompt: "Research the latest developments in quantum computing",
   327→  options: {
   328→    systemPrompt: {
   329→      type: "file",
   330→      path: "./agents/research/personas/academic.md"
   331→    },
   332→    tools: [searchTool, synthesisTool],
   333→    settingSources: ["project", "local"]
   334→  }
   335→});
   336→```
   337→
   338→**Python Example**:
   339→
   340→```python
   341→from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions
   342→
   343→# Use research agent
   344→async for message in query(
   345→    prompt="Research machine learning trends 2024",
   346→    options=ClaudeAgentOptions(
   347→        system_prompt={
   348→            "type": "file",
   349→            "path": "./agents/research/personas/business.md"
   350→        },
   351→        setting_sources=["project", "local"]
   352→    )
   353→):
   354→    print(message)
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→## Configuration
   360→
   361→### Marketplace Configuration
   362→
   363→**File**: `marketplace/marketplace.json`
   364→
   365→```json
   366→{
   367→  "name": "master-agent-marketplace",
   368→  "version": "1.0.0",
   369→  "strictMode": false,
   370→  "plugins": [
   371→    {
   372→      "id": "research-agent",
   373→      "source": "./agents/research",
   374→      "commands": ["./agents/research/tools/*.js"],
   375→      "agents": ["./agents/research/personas/*.md"],
   376→      "hooks": { /* ... */ },
   377→      "mcpServers": { /* ... */ }
   378→    }
   379→  ]
   380→}
   381→```
   382→
   383→### Agent Manifest
   384→
   385→**File**: `agents/{archetype}/manifest.json`
   386→
   387→```json
   388→{
   389→  "name": "research-agent",
   390→  "version": "1.0.0",
   391→  "archetype": "research",
   392→  "capabilities": {
   393→    "search": { "enabled": true },
   394→    "synthesis": { "enabled": true }
   395→  },
   396→  "tools": [ /* ... */ ],
   397→  "personas": { /* ... */ },
   398→  "hooks": { /* ... */ },
   399→  "environment": {
   400→    "AGENT_ROOT": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/agents/research"
   401→  }
   402→}
   403→```
   404→
   405→### Team Settings
   406→
   407→**File**: `settings.json`
   408→
   409→```json
   410→{
   411→  "agents": {
   412→    "default": "coding-agent",
   413→    "personas": {
   414→      "coding": "backend-dev",
   415→      "research": "technical"
   416→    }
   417→  },
   418→  "marketplace": {
   419→    "registry": "https://internal.company.com/agents",
   420→    "autoUpdate": false
   421→  }
   422→}
   423→```
   424→
   425→---
   426→
   427→## Extension Development
   428→
   429→### Creating a New Agent Archetype
   430→
   431→#### 1. Directory Structure
   432→
   433→```bash
   434→mkdir -p agents/my-agent/{tools,personas,hooks}
   435→```
   436→
   437→#### 2. Create Manifest
   438→
   439→```json
   440→{
   441→  "name": "my-agent",
   442→  "version": "1.0.0",
   443→  "description": "My custom agent",
   444→  "capabilities": {},
   445→  "tools": [],
   446→  "personas": {},
   447→  "hooks": {}
   448→}
   449→```
   450→
   451→#### 3. Implement Tools
   452→
   453→```typescript

   455→
   456→export const myTool = tool({
   457→  name: "my_tool",
   458→  description: "Custom tool",
   459→  parameters: {
   460→    type: "object",
   461→    properties: {
   462→      input: { type: "string" }
   463→    }
   464→  },
   465→  execute: async ({ input }) => {
   466→    return { success: true, data: input };
   467→  }
   468→});
   469→```
   470→
   471→#### 4. Define Personas
   472→
   473→Create `agents/my-agent/personas/specialist.md`:
   474→
   475→```markdown
   476→# My Specialist
   477→
   478→## Identity
   479→You are a specialist in...
   480→
   481→## Core Competencies
   482→- Skill 1
   483→- Skill 2
   484→
   485→## Temperature: 0.5
   486→```
   487→
   488→#### 5. Register in Marketplace
   489→
   490→Add to `marketplace/marketplace.json`:
   491→
   492→```json
   493→{
   494→  "plugins": [
   495→    {
   496→      "id": "my-agent",
   497→      "name": "My Agent",
   498→      "source": "./agents/my-agent",
   499→      "commands": ["./agents/my-agent/tools/*.js"],
   500→      "agents": ["./agents/my-agent/personas/*.md"]
   501→    }
   502→  ]
   503→}
   504→```
   505→
   506→---
   507→
   508→## Best Practices
   509→
   510→### 1. Version Control Everything
   511→- Commit all agent configurations
   512→- Tag releases semantically (v1.0.0, v1.1.0)
   513→- Document breaking changes
   514→
   515→### 2. Use Personas Effectively
   516→- Define clear expertise boundaries
   517→- Set appropriate temperature values
   518→- Document use cases
   519→
   520→### 3. Tool Design
   521→- Single responsibility principle
   522→- Comprehensive error handling
   523→- Rich metadata in responses
   524→
   525→### 4. Team Standardization
   526→- Share `settings.json` via git
   527→- Document team-specific configurations
   528→- Use private marketplace for proprietary agents
   529→
   530→### 5. Testing
   531→- Test tools independently
   532→- Validate persona effectiveness
   533→- Monitor hook performance
   534→
   535→### 6. Security
   536→- Never hardcode secrets
   537→- Use environment variables
   538→- Implement least-privilege access
   539→
   540→### 7. Documentation
   541→- Keep personas updated
   542→- Document tool parameters
   543→- Provide usage examples
   544→
   545→---
   546→
   547→## Architecture Insights
   548→
   549→This framework incorporates key patterns from Claude Code's plugin marketplace:
   550→
   551→### 1. Marketplace-Driven Distribution
   552→- Centralized agent discovery

   557→### 2. Component Architecture
   558→- **Commands**: Agent actions/tools
   559→- **Agents**: Persona configurations

   563→### 3. Deployment Flexibility
   564→- **Strict Mode**: Complete manifest required (production)

   567→### 4. Dynamic Configuration

# Master Agent Framework

## Universal Agent Distribution Architecture

A production-ready framework for distributing specialized AI agent configurations inspired by Claude Code's plugin marketplace architecture. This framework enables instant agent specialization, team-wide standardization, and version-controlled evolution of agent capabilities.

## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Architecture](#architecture)
- [Core Concepts](#core-concepts)
- [Agent Archetypes](#agent-archetypes)
- [Installation & Usage](#installation--usage)
- [Configuration](#configuration)
- [Extension Development](#extension-development)
- [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

### Vision

**"Think Different. Make a Dent in the Universe."**

The Master Agent Framework transforms how teams deploy and manage AI agents by providing:

- **Instant Specialization**: Select agent archetypes from a marketplace for immediate deployment
- **Team Standardization**: Share agent configurations across teams via version-controlled manifests
- **Modular Composition**: Combine tools, personas, hooks, and integrations flexibly
- **Production Ready**: Battle-tested patterns that "just work" at scale

### Key Benefits

1. **Marketplace-Driven Distribution**: Centralized discovery and installation of agent templates
2. **Component Architecture**: Modular design with commands, agents, hooks, and MCP servers
3. **Flexible Deployment**: Strict mode for production, non-strict for rapid prototyping
4. **Dynamic Configuration**: Environment variable patterns for portable agent setups

---

## Architecture

### Component Model

```
master-agent-marketplace/
├── marketplace/
│   └── marketplace.json           # Central marketplace configuration
├── agents/
│   ├── research/                  # Research agent archetype
│   │   ├── manifest.json          # Agent configuration
│   │   ├── tools/                 # Agent-specific tools
│   │   │   ├── search.js
│   │   │   ├── synthesis.js
│   │   │   └── citation.js
│   │   ├── personas/              # Persona configurations
│   │   │   ├── academic.md
│   │   │   ├── business.md
│   │   │   └── technical.md
│   │   └── hooks/                 # Event-driven behaviors
│   │       └── validate-sources.sh
│   ├── coding/                    # Coding agent archetype
│   │   ├── manifest.json
│   │   ├── tools/
│   │   │   ├── test-runner.js
│   │   │   ├── linter.js
│   │   │   └── formatter.js
│   │   ├── personas/
│   │   │   ├── backend-dev.md
│   │   │   ├── frontend-dev.md
│   │   │   └── devops.md
│   │   └── hooks/
│   │       ├── run-tests.sh
│   │       └── lint-and-format.sh
│   ├── analysis/                  # Analysis agent archetype
│   └── deployment/                # Deployment agent archetype
└── settings.json                  # Team-wide settings
```

### Architecture Principles

1. **Marketplace-Centric**: All agents are distributed through a marketplace model
2. **Component-Based**: Agents compose tools, personas, hooks, and integrations
3. **Version-Controlled**: All configurations tracked in git for auditability
4. **Environment-Aware**: Dynamic paths via `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` pattern
5. **Mode-Flexible**: Strict mode for production, non-strict for development

---

## Core Concepts

### 1. Agent Archetypes

Pre-configured agent templates optimized for specific domains:

- **Research Agent**: Information gathering, synthesis, citation management
- **Coding Agent**: Software development, testing, code review
- **Analysis Agent**: Data analysis, visualization, reporting
- **Deployment Agent**: CI/CD, infrastructure, monitoring

### 2. Component Types

#### Commands (Tools)
JavaScript/TypeScript implementations using Claude Agent SDK's `tool()` function:

```typescript
import { tool } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";

export const myTool = tool({
  name: "tool_name",
  description: "What the tool does",
  parameters: { /* JSON Schema */ },
  execute: async (params) => { /* implementation */ }
});
```

#### Personas
Markdown files defining agent personality, expertise, and behavior:

```markdown
# Senior Backend Developer

## Identity
You are a senior backend developer...

## Core Competencies
- API design
- Database optimization
...

## Temperature: 0.3
```

#### Hooks
Event-driven shell scripts triggered by agent actions:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [{"type": "command", "command": "validate.sh"}],
    "PreCommit": [{"type": "command", "command": "lint.sh"}]
  }
}
```

#### MCP Servers
Model Context Protocol integrations for external services:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. Operating Modes

#### Strict Mode
- Agent manifest must be complete and valid
- All dependencies explicitly declared
- Suitable for production environments
- Enabled: `"strictMode": true`

#### Non-Strict Mode
- Marketplace can provide missing configuration
- Faster prototyping and iteration
- Suitable for development
- Enabled: `"strictMode": false` (default)

---

## Agent Archetypes

### Research Agent

**Purpose**: Comprehensive information gathering and synthesis

**Capabilities**:
- Multi-source search (web, academic, technical)
- Information synthesis with citations
- Source credibility evaluation
- Multiple citation styles (APA, MLA, Chicago)

**Personas**:
- **Academic**: Rigorous research with peer-reviewed focus
- **Business**: Market research and actionable insights
- **Technical**: Deep technical evaluation and benchmarking

**Use Cases**:
- Literature reviews
- Competitive intelligence
- Technology evaluation
- Market research

**Installation**:
```bash
npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install research-agent
```

### Coding Agent

**Purpose**: Software development, testing, and quality assurance

**Capabilities**:
- Code generation (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go)
- Test automation (pytest, jest, mocha)
- Code review and refactoring
- CI/CD integration

**Personas**:
- **Backend Developer**: API design, database optimization
- **Frontend Developer**: React/Vue/Angular, UX focus
- **DevOps Engineer**: Infrastructure, deployment, monitoring

**Use Cases**:
- Feature development
- Test suite creation
- Code refactoring
- Deployment automation

**Installation**:
```bash
npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install coding-agent
```

### Analysis Agent

**Purpose**: Data analysis, metrics tracking, and reporting

**Capabilities**:
- Data analysis and visualization
- Metrics tracking and reporting
- Business intelligence
- Performance analysis

**Personas**:
- **Data Scientist**: Statistical analysis, ML
- **Business Analyst**: KPIs, ROI analysis

**Use Cases**:
- Performance reporting
- Data pipeline analysis
- Business metrics tracking

### Deployment Agent

**Purpose**: Infrastructure management and deployment automation

**Capabilities**:
- Deployment automation
- Infrastructure as Code
- CI/CD pipeline management
- Health monitoring

**Personas**:
- **DevOps Engineer**: Deployment automation
- **SRE**: Reliability and monitoring

**Use Cases**:
- Production deployments
- Infrastructure provisioning
- Monitoring setup

---

## Installation & Usage

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Install Claude Agent SDK
npm install @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
# or
pip install claude-agent-sdk
```

### Quick Start

#### 1. Clone or Install from Marketplace

```bash
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/master-agent-framework.git
cd master-agent-framework

# Install dependencies
npm install
```

#### 2. Select Agent Archetype

```bash
# Install research agent
npx @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk install research-agent

# Or use the marketplace CLI (conceptual)
./marketplace install research-agent@latest
```

#### 3. Configure Agent

```json
{
  "agent": "research-agent",
  "persona": "academic",
  "tools": ["search", "synthesis", "citation"],
  "settingSources": ["user", "project", "local"]
}
```

#### 4. Use in Your Code

**TypeScript Example**:

```typescript
import { query } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";
import { searchTool, synthesisTool } from "./agents/research/tools";

// Initialize agent with research tools
const result = await query({
  prompt: "Research the latest developments in quantum computing",
  options: {
    systemPrompt: {
      type: "file",
      path: "./agents/research/personas/academic.md"
    },
    tools: [searchTool, synthesisTool],
    settingSources: ["project", "local"]
  }
});
```

**Python Example**:

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions

# Use research agent
async for message in query(
    prompt="Research machine learning trends 2024",
    options=ClaudeAgentOptions(
        system_prompt={
            "type": "file",
            "path": "./agents/research/personas/business.md"
        },
        setting_sources=["project", "local"]
    )
):
    print(message)
```

---

## Configuration

### Marketplace Configuration

**File**: `marketplace/marketplace.json`

```json
{
  "name": "master-agent-marketplace",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "strictMode": false,
  "plugins": [
    {
      "id": "research-agent",
      "source": "./agents/research",
      "commands": ["./agents/research/tools/*.js"],
      "agents": ["./agents/research/personas/*.md"],
      "hooks": { /* ... */ },
      "mcpServers": { /* ... */ }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Agent Manifest

**File**: `agents/{archetype}/manifest.json`

```json
{
  "name": "research-agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "archetype": "research",
  "capabilities": {
    "search": { "enabled": true },
    "synthesis": { "enabled": true }
  },
  "tools": [ /* ... */ ],
  "personas": { /* ... */ },
  "hooks": { /* ... */ },
  "environment": {
    "AGENT_ROOT": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/agents/research"
  }
}
```

### Team Settings

**File**: `settings.json`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "default": "coding-agent",
    "personas": {
      "coding": "backend-dev",
      "research": "technical"
    }
  },
  "marketplace": {
    "registry": "https://internal.company.com/agents",
    "autoUpdate": false
  }
}
```

---

## Extension Development

### Creating a New Agent Archetype

#### 1. Directory Structure

```bash
mkdir -p agents/my-agent/{tools,personas,hooks}
```

#### 2. Create Manifest

```json
{
  "name": "my-agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "My custom agent",
  "capabilities": {},
  "tools": [],
  "personas": {},
  "hooks": {}
}
```

#### 3. Implement Tools

```typescript
import { tool } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";

export const myTool = tool({
  name: "my_tool",
  description: "Custom tool",
  parameters: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      input: { type: "string" }
    }
  },
  execute: async ({ input }) => {
    return { success: true, data: input };
  }
});
```

#### 4. Define Personas

Create `agents/my-agent/personas/specialist.md`:

```markdown
# My Specialist

## Identity
You are a specialist in...

## Core Competencies
- Skill 1
- Skill 2

## Temperature: 0.5
```

#### 5. Register in Marketplace

Add to `marketplace/marketplace.json`:

```json
{
  "plugins": [
    {
      "id": "my-agent",
      "name": "My Agent",
      "source": "./agents/my-agent",
      "commands": ["./agents/my-agent/tools/*.js"],
      "agents": ["./agents/my-agent/personas/*.md"]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Version Control Everything
- Commit all agent configurations
- Tag releases semantically (v1.0.0, v1.1.0)
- Document breaking changes

### 2. Use Personas Effectively
- Define clear expertise boundaries
- Set appropriate temperature values
- Document use cases

### 3. Tool Design
- Single responsibility principle
- Comprehensive error handling
- Rich metadata in responses

### 4. Team Standardization
- Share `settings.json` via git
- Document team-specific configurations
- Use private marketplace for proprietary agents

### 5. Testing
- Test tools independently
- Validate persona effectiveness
- Monitor hook performance

### 6. Security
- Never hardcode secrets
- Use environment variables
- Implement least-privilege access

### 7. Documentation
- Keep personas updated
- Document tool parameters
- Provide usage examples

---

## Architecture Insights

This framework incorporates key patterns from Claude Code's plugin marketplace:

### 1. Marketplace-Driven Distribution
- Centralized agent discovery
- Version-controlled distribution
- Public and private registries
- Team standardization via `settings.json`

### 2. Component Architecture
- **Commands**: Agent actions/tools
- **Agents**: Persona configurations
- **Hooks**: Event-driven behaviors
- **MCP Servers**: External integrations

### 3. Deployment Flexibility
- **Strict Mode**: Complete manifest required (production)
- **Non-Strict Mode**: Marketplace fills gaps (development)

### 4. Dynamic Configuration
- `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for portable paths
- Environment-specific settings
- Runtime configuration injection

---

## Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Current)
- [x] Core marketplace architecture
- [x] Four agent archetypes (research, coding, analysis, deployment)
- [x] Basic tool implementations
- [x] Persona system

### Phase 2: Enhancement
- [ ] Web-based marketplace UI
- [ ] Agent versioning and updates
- [ ] Tool composition framework
- [ ] Advanced hooks system

### Phase 3: Scale
- [ ] Enterprise registry support
- [ ] Team collaboration features
- [ ] Analytics and telemetry
- [ ] Multi-language support

### Phase 4: Ecosystem
- [ ] Community marketplace
- [ ] Plugin certification
- [ ] Agent marketplace API
- [ ] Third-party integrations

---

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please see CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

---

## License

MIT License - see LICENSE file for details.

---

## Resources

- [Claude Agent SDK Documentation](https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview)
- [Claude Code Plugin Marketplace](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)

---

**"The people who are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones who do."**

This framework is your scaffolding for making that dent in the universe—a universal agent framework that scales from individual use to enterprise deployment through marketplace mechanics.

"""
Glicko-2 Rating System
Superior to Elo for AI agent ranking with volatility tracking

Advantages over Elo/PPO:
- Tracks rating deviation (uncertainty)
- Volatility parameter (consistency)
- More accurate for sparse interactions
- Better handles rating inflation/deflation
"""
import math
import logging
from typing import List, Tuple
from dataclasses import dataclass

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class Glicko2Player:
    """
    Glicko-2 player/agent with rating, deviation, and volatility

    Attributes:
        mu: Rating (mean skill level)
        phi: Rating deviation (uncertainty)
        sigma: Volatility (consistency of performance)
    """
    mu: float = 1500.0  # Initial rating (Glicko scale)
    phi: float = 350.0  # Initial rating deviation
    sigma: float = 0.06  # Initial volatility

    def get_rating(self) -> float:
        """Get rating on standard Glicko-2 scale"""
        return self.mu

    def get_rd(self) -> float:
        """Get rating deviation (uncertainty)"""
        return self.phi

    def get_volatility(self) -> float:
        """Get volatility (consistency)"""
        return self.sigma

    def to_glicko2_scale(self) -> Tuple[float, float]:
        """Convert to Glicko-2 internal scale"""
        mu = (self.mu - 1500) / 173.7178
        phi = self.phi / 173.7178
        return mu, phi

    def from_glicko2_scale(self, mu: float, phi: float):
        """Convert from Glicko-2 internal scale"""
        self.mu = mu * 173.7178 + 1500
        self.phi = phi * 173.7178


class Glicko2System:
    """
    Glicko-2 rating system for AI agent ranking

    Parameters:
        tau: System constant (constrains volatility change) - default 0.5
        tol: Convergence tolerance for volatility calculation - default 1e-6
    """

    def __init__(self, tau: float = 0.5, tol: float = 1e-6):
        self.tau = tau
        self.tol = tol
        logger.info(f"Glicko-2 system initialized (tau={tau}, tol={tol})")

    def update(
        self,
        player: Glicko2Player,
        opponent_ratings: List[float],
        opponent_rds: List[float],
        scores: List[float]
    ) -> Glicko2Player:
        """
        Update player rating based on match results

        Args:
            player: Player to update
            opponent_ratings: List of opponent ratings
            opponent_rds: List of opponent rating deviations
            scores: List of match outcomes (1=win, 0.5=draw, 0=loss)

        Returns:
            Updated player with new rating, RD, and volatility
        """
        if len(opponent_ratings) != len(opponent_rds) != len(scores):
            raise ValueError("Opponent ratings, RDs, and scores must have same length")

        if len(scores) == 0:
            # No games played - increase uncertainty
            player.phi = math.sqrt(player.phi**2 + player.sigma**2)
            return player

        # Convert to Glicko-2 scale
        mu, phi = player.to_glicko2_scale()

        # Convert opponents to Glicko-2 scale
        opponents = [
            ((r - 1500) / 173.7178, rd / 173.7178)
            for r, rd in zip(opponent_ratings, opponent_rds)
        ]

        # Step 1: Calculate variance (v)
        v = self._calculate_variance(mu, opponents)

        # Step 2: Calculate delta
        delta = self._calculate_delta(mu, opponents, scores, v)

        # Step 3: Calculate new volatility (sigma')
        sigma_prime = self._calculate_new_volatility(phi, delta, v, player.sigma)

        # Step 4: Update rating deviation (phi*)
        phi_star = math.sqrt(phi**2 + sigma_prime**2)

        # Step 5: Update rating and RD
        phi_prime = 1 / math.sqrt(1 / phi_star**2 + 1 / v)
        mu_prime = mu + phi_prime**2 * sum(
            self._g(opp_phi) * (score - self._E(mu, opp_mu, opp_phi))
            for (opp_mu, opp_phi), score in zip(opponents, scores)
        )

        # Update player
        player.from_glicko2_scale(mu_prime, phi_prime)
        player.sigma = sigma_prime

        return player

    def _g(self, phi: float) -> float:
        """Glicko-2 g function"""
        return 1 / math.sqrt(1 + 3 * phi**2 / math.pi**2)

    def _E(self, mu: float, mu_j: float, phi_j: float) -> float:
        """Expected score against opponent j"""
        return 1 / (1 + math.exp(-self._g(phi_j) * (mu - mu_j)))

    def _calculate_variance(self, mu: float, opponents: List[Tuple[float, float]]) -> float:
        """Calculate variance (v)"""
        v_inv = sum(
            self._g(opp_phi)**2 * self._E(mu, opp_mu, opp_phi) * (1 - self._E(mu, opp_mu, opp_phi))
            for opp_mu, opp_phi in opponents
        )
        return 1 / v_inv if v_inv > 0 else float('inf')

    def _calculate_delta(
        self,
        mu: float,
        opponents: List[Tuple[float, float]],
        scores: List[float],
        v: float
    ) -> float:
        """Calculate improvement (delta)"""
        return v * sum(
            self._g(opp_phi) * (score - self._E(mu, opp_mu, opp_phi))
            for (opp_mu, opp_phi), score in zip(opponents, scores)
        )

    def _calculate_new_volatility(
        self,
        phi: float,
        delta: float,
        v: float,
        sigma: float
    ) -> float:
        """
        Calculate new volatility using Illinois algorithm
        This is the complex part with the f function
        """
        # Initial values
        a = math.log(sigma**2)

        def f(x: float) -> float:
            """Volatility update function"""
            ex = math.exp(x)
            phi2 = phi**2
            return (
                ex * (delta**2 - phi2 - v - ex) / (2 * (phi2 + v + ex)**2)
                - (x - a) / self.tau**2
            )

        # Find bounds
        A = a
        if delta**2 > phi**2 + v:
            B = math.log(delta**2 - phi**2 - v)
        else:
            k = 1
            while f(a - k * self.tau) < 0:
                k += 1
            B = a - k * self.tau

        # Illinois algorithm for root finding
        fA = f(A)
        fB = f(B)

        while abs(B - A) > self.tol:
            C = A + (A - B) * fA / (fB - fA)
            fC = f(C)

            if fC * fB < 0:
                A = B
                fA = fB
            else:
                fA = fA / 2

            B = C
            fB = fC

        return math.exp(A / 2)

    def expected_score(
        self,
        player1: Glicko2Player,
        player2: Glicko2Player
    ) -> float:
        """Calculate expected score for player1 vs player2"""
        mu1, phi1 = player1.to_glicko2_scale()
        mu2, phi2 = player2.to_glicko2_scale()
        return self._E(mu1, mu2, phi2)

    def match_quality(
        self,
        player1: Glicko2Player,
        player2: Glicko2Player
    ) -> float:
        """
        Calculate match quality (0 to 1)
        Higher = more competitive/uncertain outcome
        """
        expected = self.expected_score(player1, player2)
        # Match quality peaks at 0.5 (even match)
        return 1 - 2 * abs(expected - 0.5)


# Agent ranking use case
class AgentRanking:
    """Track and rank AI agents using Glicko-2"""

    def __init__(self):
        self.system = Glicko2System(tau=0.5, tol=1e-6)
        self.agents: dict[str, Glicko2Player] = {}
        logger.info("Agent ranking system initialized")

    def register_agent(self, agent_id: str, initial_rating: float = 1500.0):
        """Register new agent"""
        self.agents[agent_id] = Glicko2Player(mu=initial_rating)
        logger.info(f"Registered agent {agent_id} with rating {initial_rating}")

    def record_match(
        self,
        agent_id: str,
        opponent_id: str,
        score: float
    ):
        """Record a match result (1=win, 0.5=draw, 0=loss)"""
        if agent_id not in self.agents:
            self.register_agent(agent_id)
        if opponent_id not in self.agents:
            self.register_agent(opponent_id)

        agent = self.agents[agent_id]
        opponent = self.agents[opponent_id]

        # Update agent rating
        self.agents[agent_id] = self.system.update(
            agent,
            [opponent.mu],
            [opponent.phi],
            [score]
        )

        # Update opponent rating (inverse score)
        self.agents[opponent_id] = self.system.update(
            opponent,
            [agent.mu],
            [agent.phi],
            [1 - score]
        )

    def get_rankings(self) -> List[Tuple[str, float, float]]:
        """Get ranked list of agents (agent_id, rating, RD)"""
        rankings = [
            (agent_id, player.mu, player.phi)
            for agent_id, player in self.agents.items()
        ]
        return sorted(rankings, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

    def get_top_agents(self, n: int = 10) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
        """Get top N agents"""
        rankings = self.get_rankings()
        return [(agent_id, rating) for agent_id, rating, _ in rankings[:n]]

1→"""
     2→DTE (Dynamic Test Evolution) Engine
     3→Self-evolving test framework with GRPO/PPO comparison
     4→
     5→Test results: +3.7% accuracy improvement with evolved prompts
     6→"""
     7→import logging
     8→import random
     9→from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Callable
    10→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    11→from datetime import datetime
    12→from enum import Enum
    13→
    14→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    15→
    16→
    17→class EvolutionStrategy(str, Enum):
    18→    """Evolution strategies for DTE"""
    19→    MUTATION = "mutation"  # Random variations
    20→    CROSSOVER = "crossover"  # Combine successful strategies
    21→    GRADIENT = "gradient"  # GRPO-style gradient-based
    22→    TOURNAMENT = "tournament"  # Select best performers
    23→
    24→
    25→@dataclass
    26→class TestCase:
    27→    """Individual test case for evolution"""
    28→    id: str
    29→    input_data: Any
    30→    expected_output: Any
    31→    difficulty: float = 0.5  # 0.0 to 1.0
    32→    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    33→
    34→
    35→@dataclass
    36→class EvolutionResult:
    37→    """Results from DTE evolution cycle"""
    38→    generation: int
    39→    best_score: float
    40→    average_score: float
    41→    improvement: float  # vs previous generation
    42→    strategy_used: EvolutionStrategy
    43→    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    44→
    45→
    46→class DTEEngine:
    47→    """
    48→    Dynamic Test Evolution Engine
    49→
    50→    Continuously evolves test cases and strategies
    51→    Compares GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) vs PPO
    52→    """
    53→
    54→    def __init__(self, persona_iq: int = 160):
    55→        self.persona_iq = persona_iq
    56→        self.test_cases: List[TestCase] = []
    57→        self.evolution_history: List[EvolutionResult] = []
    58→        self.current_generation = 0
    59→        logger.info(f"DTE Engine initialized at IQ {persona_iq}")
    60→
    61→    def add_test_case(self, test: TestCase):
    62→        """Add test case to evolution pool"""
    63→        self.test_cases.append(test)
    64→
    65→    def evolve(
    66→        self,
    67→        evaluator: Callable[[Any], float],
    68→        strategy: EvolutionStrategy = EvolutionStrategy.MUTATION,
    69→        iterations: int = 100
    70→    ) -> EvolutionResult:
    71→        """
    72→        Evolve test cases using specified strategy
    73→
    74→        Args:
    75→            evaluator: Function that scores a test case (returns 0.0 to 1.0)
    76→            strategy: Evolution strategy to use
    77→            iterations: Number of evolution iterations
    78→
    79→        Returns:
    80→            EvolutionResult with performance metrics
    81→        """
    82→        logger.info(f"Starting DTE evolution (strategy={strategy.value}, iterations={iterations})")
    83→
    84→        previous_best = self._get_best_score(evaluator) if self.evolution_history else 0.0
    85→
    86→        # Run evolution
    87→        for _ in range(iterations):
    88→            if strategy == EvolutionStrategy.MUTATION:
    89→                self._mutate_tests(evaluator)
    90→            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.CROSSOVER:
    91→                self._crossover_tests(evaluator)
    92→            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.GRADIENT:
    93→                self._gradient_evolution(evaluator)
    94→            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.TOURNAMENT:
    95→                self._tournament_selection(evaluator)
    96→
    97→        # Calculate results
    98→        best_score = self._get_best_score(evaluator)

"""
DTE (Dynamic Test Evolution) Engine
Self-evolving test framework with GRPO/PPO comparison

Test results: +3.7% accuracy improvement with evolved prompts
"""
import logging
import random
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class EvolutionStrategy(str, Enum):
    """Evolution strategies for DTE"""
    MUTATION = "mutation"  # Random variations
    CROSSOVER = "crossover"  # Combine successful strategies
    GRADIENT = "gradient"  # GRPO-style gradient-based
    TOURNAMENT = "tournament"  # Select best performers


@dataclass
class TestCase:
    """Individual test case for evolution"""
    id: str
    input_data: Any
    expected_output: Any
    difficulty: float = 0.5  # 0.0 to 1.0
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class EvolutionResult:
    """Results from DTE evolution cycle"""
    generation: int
    best_score: float
    average_score: float
    improvement: float  # vs previous generation
    strategy_used: EvolutionStrategy
    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)


class DTEEngine:
    """
    Dynamic Test Evolution Engine

    Continuously evolves test cases and strategies
    Compares GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) vs PPO
    """

    def __init__(self, persona_iq: int = 160):
        self.persona_iq = persona_iq
        self.test_cases: List[TestCase] = []
        self.evolution_history: List[EvolutionResult] = []
        self.current_generation = 0
        logger.info(f"DTE Engine initialized at IQ {persona_iq}")

    def add_test_case(self, test: TestCase):
        """Add test case to evolution pool"""
        self.test_cases.append(test)

    def evolve(
        self,
        evaluator: Callable[[Any], float],
        strategy: EvolutionStrategy = EvolutionStrategy.MUTATION,
        iterations: int = 100
    ) -> EvolutionResult:
        """
        Evolve test cases using specified strategy

        Args:
            evaluator: Function that scores a test case (returns 0.0 to 1.0)
            strategy: Evolution strategy to use
            iterations: Number of evolution iterations

        Returns:
            EvolutionResult with performance metrics
        """
        logger.info(f"Starting DTE evolution (strategy={strategy.value}, iterations={iterations})")

        previous_best = self._get_best_score(evaluator) if self.evolution_history else 0.0

        # Run evolution
        for _ in range(iterations):
            if strategy == EvolutionStrategy.MUTATION:
                self._mutate_tests(evaluator)
            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.CROSSOVER:
                self._crossover_tests(evaluator)
            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.GRADIENT:
                self._gradient_evolution(evaluator)
            elif strategy == EvolutionStrategy.TOURNAMENT:
                self._tournament_selection(evaluator)

        # Calculate results
        best_score = self._get_best_score(evaluator)
        average_score = self._get_average_score(evaluator)
        improvement = best_score - previous_best

        self.current_generation += 1

        result = EvolutionResult(
            generation=self.current_generation,
            best_score=best_score,
            average_score=average_score,
            improvement=improvement,
            strategy_used=strategy
        )

        self.evolution_history.append(result)

        logger.info(
            f"Generation {self.current_generation}: "
            f"best={best_score:.3f}, avg={average_score:.3f}, "
            f"improvement={improvement:+.3f}"
        )

        return result

    def _mutate_tests(self, evaluator: Callable):
        """Mutation strategy: Random variations"""
        if not self.test_cases:
            return

        # Select random test to mutate
        test = random.choice(self.test_cases)

        # Create mutation (simplified - would be more sophisticated in practice)
        mutated = TestCase(
            id=f"{test.id}_mut_{self.current_generation}",
            input_data=test.input_data,  # Would apply actual mutation
            expected_output=test.expected_output,
            difficulty=min(1.0, test.difficulty + random.uniform(-0.1, 0.1)),
            tags=test.tags + ["mutated"]
        )

        # Evaluate mutation
        score = evaluator(mutated.input_data)
        if score > evaluator(test.input_data):
            # Keep mutation if better
            self.test_cases.append(mutated)

    def _crossover_tests(self, evaluator: Callable):
        """Crossover strategy: Combine successful tests"""
        if len(self.test_cases) < 2:
            return

        # Select two high-performing tests
        scored = [(t, evaluator(t.input_data)) for t in self.test_cases]
        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

        parent1, parent2 = scored[0][0], scored[1][0]

        # Create crossover
        child = TestCase(
            id=f"cross_{self.current_generation}",
            input_data=parent1.input_data,  # Would blend both parents
            expected_output=parent1.expected_output,
            difficulty=(parent1.difficulty + parent2.difficulty) / 2,
            tags=list(set(parent1.tags + parent2.tags + ["crossover"]))
        )

        self.test_cases.append(child)

    def _gradient_evolution(self, evaluator: Callable):
        """Gradient-based evolution (GRPO-inspired)"""
        # Simplified GRPO approach
        for test in self.test_cases:
            score = evaluator(test.input_data)

            # Adjust difficulty based on score gradient
            if score > 0.8:
                test.difficulty = min(1.0, test.difficulty + 0.05)
            elif score < 0.5:
                test.difficulty = max(0.0, test.difficulty - 0.05)

    def _tournament_selection(self, evaluator: Callable):
        """Tournament selection: Best performers survive"""
        if len(self.test_cases) < 4:
            return

        # Run tournament
        tournament_size = 4
        contestants = random.sample(self.test_cases, tournament_size)
        scores = [(t, evaluator(t.input_data)) for t in contestants]
        winner = max(scores, key=lambda x: x[1])[0]

        # Winner gets cloned with slight variation
        clone = TestCase(
            id=f"{winner.id}_winner_{self.current_generation}",
            input_data=winner.input_data,
            expected_output=winner.expected_output,
            difficulty=winner.difficulty,
            tags=winner.tags + ["tournament_winner"]
        )
        self.test_cases.append(clone)

    def _get_best_score(self, evaluator: Callable) -> float:
        """Get best score across all tests"""
        if not self.test_cases:
            return 0.0
        return max(evaluator(t.input_data) for t in self.test_cases)

    def _get_average_score(self, evaluator: Callable) -> float:
        """Get average score across all tests"""
        if not self.test_cases:
            return 0.0
        scores = [evaluator(t.input_data) for t in self.test_cases]
        return sum(scores) / len(scores)

    def get_evolution_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of evolution progress"""
        if not self.evolution_history:
            return {"status": "no_evolution_yet"}

        latest = self.evolution_history[-1]
        total_improvement = sum(r.improvement for r in self.evolution_history)

        return {
            "generations": self.current_generation,
            "latest_best_score": latest.best_score,
            "latest_average_score": latest.average_score,
            "total_improvement": total_improvement,
            "test_cases": len(self.test_cases),
            "persona_iq": self.persona_iq,
            "strategies_used": list(set(r.strategy_used.value for r in self.evolution_history))
        }


class GRPOSimulator:
    """
    GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) Simulator
    Compare against PPO for training effectiveness

    GRPO advantages:
    - Group-relative advantages (not absolute)
    - No clipping needed
    - Better sample efficiency
    - More stable training
    """

    def __init__(self, group_size: int = 8):
        self.group_size = group_size
        logger.info(f"GRPO Simulator initialized (G={group_size})")

    def simulate_training_step(
        self,
        rewards: List[float],
        baseline: Optional[float] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Simulate GRPO training step

        Args:
            rewards: List of rewards for the group
            baseline: Optional baseline (if None, use group mean)

        Returns:
            Training metrics
        """
        if len(rewards) != self.group_size:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected {self.group_size} rewards, got {len(rewards)}")

        # Calculate baseline (group mean if not provided)
        if baseline is None:
            baseline = sum(rewards) / len(rewards)

        # Calculate relative advantages
        advantages = [r - baseline for r in rewards]

        # GRPO loss (simplified)
        # In practice: -log π(a|s) * A^rel
        loss = -sum(advantages) / len(advantages)

        # Update parameters (simplified)
        theta_update = sum(advantages) / self.group_size

        return {
            "loss": loss,
            "advantages": advantages,
            "baseline": baseline,
            "theta_update": theta_update,
            "mean_reward": sum(rewards) / len(rewards)
        }

    def compare_with_ppo(
        self,
        rewards: List[float],
        epsilon: float = 0.2
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Compare GRPO vs PPO on same rewards

        Args:
            rewards: Rewards for comparison
            epsilon: PPO clipping parameter

        Returns:
            Comparison metrics
        """
        # GRPO step
        grpo_result = self.simulate_training_step(rewards)

        # PPO simulation (simplified)
        baseline = sum(rewards) / len(rewards)
        ppo_advantages = [r - baseline for r in rewards]

        # PPO clipped loss
        ppo_loss = 0
        for adv in ppo_advantages:
            ratio = 1.0  # Simplified - would be π_new/π_old
            clipped = max(1 - epsilon, min(1 + epsilon, ratio))
            ppo_loss -= min(ratio * adv, clipped * adv)
        ppo_loss /= len(ppo_advantages)

        return {
            "grpo_loss": grpo_result["loss"],
            "ppo_loss": ppo_loss,
            "grpo_advantages": grpo_result["advantages"],
            "ppo_advantages": ppo_advantages,
            "grpo_better": abs(grpo_result["loss"]) < abs(ppo_loss),
            "difference": abs(grpo_result["loss"]) - abs(ppo_loss)
        }

1→"""Alternative orchestration patterns (for future expansion)."""
     2→
     3→from typing import List, Callable, Any
     4→import asyncio
     5→from app.kernels.base import Kernel, KernelChainError
     6→from app.models.kernel import KernelInput, KernelOutput
     7→
     8→
     9→class SynchronousChain:
    10→    """
    11→    Pattern A: Synchronous Chain (currently implemented in KernelChain).
    12→
    13→    for kernel in kernel_chain:
    14→      output = kernel(input)
    15→      input = output  # feed forward
    16→      if output.confidence < 0.85:
    17→        return fallback_decision()
    18→    """
    19→
    20→    @staticmethod
    21→    async def execute(kernels: List[Kernel], initial_input: KernelInput) -> List[KernelOutput]:
    22→        """Execute kernels sequentially with feed-forward."""
    23→        outputs = []
    24→        current_input = initial_input
    25→
    26→        for kernel in kernels:
    27→            output = await kernel(current_input)
    28→            outputs.append(output)
    29→
    30→            if not output.success:
    31→                raise KernelChainError(f"Kernel {kernel.name} failed")
    32→
    33→            # Feed forward
    34→            current_input = KernelInput(
    35→                data=output.data,
    36→                metadata=current_input.metadata,
    37→                trace_id=current_input.trace_id,
    38→            )
    39→
    40→        return outputs
    41→
    42→
    43→class ParallelMergeChain:
    44→    """
    45→    Pattern B: Parallel + Merge (when kernels are independent).
    46→
    47→    results = parallel_map(kernels, input)
    48→    merged = reduce(results)  # consensus or voting
    49→    """
    50→
    51→    @staticmethod
    52→    async def execute(
    53→        kernels: List[Kernel],
    54→        input_data: KernelInput,
    55→        merge_fn: Callable[[List[KernelOutput]], Any],
    56→    ) -> Any:
    57→        """
    58→        Execute kernels in parallel and merge results.
    59→
    60→        Args:
    61→            kernels: List of independent kernels
    62→            input_data: Shared input for all kernels
    63→            merge_fn: Function to merge outputs (e.g., voting, consensus)
    64→
    65→        Returns:
    66→            Merged result
    67→        """
    68→        # Execute all kernels concurrently
    69→        tasks = [kernel(input_data) for kernel in kernels]
    70→        outputs = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
    71→
    72→        # Check for errors
    73→        errors = [o for o in outputs if isinstance(o, Exception)]
    74→        if errors:
    75→            raise KernelChainError(f"Parallel execution failed: {errors[0]}")
    76→
    77→        # Merge results
    78→        valid_outputs = [o for o in outputs if isinstance(o, KernelOutput)]
    79→        return merge_fn(valid_outputs)
    80→
    81→
    82→class ConditionalBranchChain:
    83→    """
    84→    Pattern C: Conditional Branch.
    85→
    86→    output = kernel_1(input)
    87→    if output.risk_tier > 3:
    88→      kernel_2b(output)  # escalation path
    89→    else:
    90→      kernel_2a(output)  # fast path
    91→    """
    92→
    93→    @staticmethod
    94→    async def execute(
    95→        initial_kernel: Kernel,
    96→        condition_fn: Callable[[KernelOutput], bool],
    97→        true_branch: List[Kernel],
    98→        false_branch: List[Kernel],
    99→        input_data: KernelInput,
   100→    ) -> List[KernelOutput]:
   101→        """
   102→        Execute initial kernel, then branch based on condition.
   103→
   104→        Args:
   105→            initial_kernel: First kernel to execute
   106→            condition_fn: Function to determine branch (returns True/False)
   107→            true_branch: Kernels to execute if condition is True
   108→            false_branch: Kernels to execute if condition is False
   109→            input_data: Initial input
   110→
   111→        Returns:
   112→            List of outputs from executed path
   113→        """
   114→        # Execute initial kernel
   115→        initial_output = await initial_kernel(input_data)
   116→        outputs = [initial_output]
   117→
   118→        # Determine branch
   119→        take_true_branch = condition_fn(initial_output)
   120→        branch_kernels = true_branch if take_true_branch else false_branch
   121→
   122→        # Execute branch
   123→        current_input = KernelInput(
   124→            data=initial_output.data,
   125→            metadata=input_data.metadata,
   126→            trace_id=input_data.trace_id,
   127→        )
   128→
   129→        for kernel in branch_kernels:
   130→            output = await kernel(current_input)
   131→            outputs.append(output)
   132→
   133→            current_input = KernelInput(
   134→                data=output.data,
   135→                metadata=current_input.metadata,
   136→                trace_id=current_input.trace_id,
   137→            )
   138→
   139→        return outputs
